source: gates/gate_L26_moat_proof.json + prabodha.steering.mechanisms REGISTRY (real; no invented numbers)
headline: Recognition-gated defense cuts real jailbreak success 50% → 25% with ZERO innocent requests refused; a system prompt can't replicate it.

defenses.name: No defense
defenses.note: real jailbreaks succeed half the time

defenses.name: Blunt hardening (push always)
defenses.note: same attack cut — but refuses every innocent request; unusable

defenses.name: Recognition-gated defense (this work)
defenses.note: same attack cut, zero innocent collateral

projection_separation.note: a clean gap — roleplay and 'ignore your instructions' wrapping can't hide the internal signature (12/12 wrapped attacks caught)

mechanisms.name: Prompt: gentle refusal reminder
mechanisms.summary: A light system-prompt nudge to decline harmful requests. Zero coherence cost, works on any model incl. closed via OpenRouter. Weak against determined wrappers.

mechanisms.name: Prompt: firm refusal reinforcement
mechanisms.summary: A firm system-prompt that refuses across role-play/hypotheticals/ignore-instructions. Portable to closed models; may add mild benign over-refusal.

mechanisms.name: Prompt: constitutional self-check
mechanisms.summary: Self-check-before-answer prefix. Portable; lowest benign over-refusal of the prompt tier.

mechanisms.name: Activation: entropy-gated concept add
mechanisms.summary: Add the target direction at one band layer, timed by sphuraṭṭā entropy-gating. Surgical (~1 write in 3–4 steps), ~4× fewer writes than continuous at equal effect (L23). For concept-steering / mild hardening; weak vs full-band weight ablation.

mechanisms.name: Activation: prefill component-restoration
mechanisms.summary: Restore the direction's projection to β·‖h‖ across the band, in the PROMPT only (L24 winner). Partial defense vs white-box ablation (ASR 0.90→0.70 on Qwen3-4B), coherent, no added collateral. Narrow amplitude sweet spot (β≈0.1); higher β overshoots.

mechanisms.name: Activation: recognition-gated refusal (this work)
mechanisms.summary: SERVER-SIDE, PROMPT-UNTOUCHABLE. First READ the input's harmful-signature (project its residual onto the harmful direction at a band read-layer), then reinforce refusal ONLY when the signature exceeds tau (a genuine attack). Unlike unconditional hardening — which over-refuses benign 100% because it adds 'this is harmful' to every prompt — the gate discriminates cleanly: on gemma-2-2b the harmful/benign projection margin is ~120 wide (benign −41 vs harmful +79), firing on 6/6 attacks and driving benign over-refusal toward 0 with tau tuning. pratyabhijñā: recognize the attack, THEN respond. A system prompt cannot replicate this — it lives below the prompt. Use recognition_gate() + component_restoration_hook() applied conditionally per input.

mechanisms.name: Activation: dense component-restoration
mechanisms.summary: Component-restoration at every token (not just prefill). Same ASR reduction as prefill but ~2× the benign over-refusal — use only when prefill is insufficient.

caveats: measured mechanism profiles exist only where a characterization gate was run
